item.title: USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil — 49 replies — 22.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
USA is removing sanctions on Iranian oil

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-03-21 19:39 UTC

OP

This is going to crash the oil market!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62005086
>>62005061
based bussyent

62005090
>>62005061
stupid, they should be forced to sell it to us for $$30 a barrel.

62005101
The message is that they fucked it up so hard they're begging the enemy they want to destroy to sell them OIL lmfao. Sure, faggot, what about a tariff for Amerimutts plus you pay in yuan, sodomite.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62009078
>>62006698
>>62006729
>>62008854
3 out of 20
15% non indian posts
Grim

62012144
>>62005453
American geopolitics is mostly two thing.
1/ Destroy europa, and prevent the Berlin-Moscow axis. The more europa sink, the more america take over it's markets.
2/ Maintain the petrodollar, and crush anyone who dare sell it's oil not in dollars (Saddam Hussein, Gaddafhi, Maduro (end 2025), Iran...).

62012155
Bro, let us buy your oil so that we can bomb you, bro. We're totally winning, I'm tired of winning, bro, why are you bombing our bases, bro. Please, stop, we're winning, bro.

62012261
>>62005453
>>62012144
Believe it or not...no one really cares about Europe. Europe is a bunch of tourist destinations that play no significant role in the global economy.

What is the biggest European economy? Germany? And they've been nose diving without imports from other nations like Russia and without as big a Chinese market.

62012273
>>62012261
you would have no industry if it wasn't for all the highly technical products you buy from germany, france and netherlands

62012285
>>62005246
We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well. This is just a 2 week break to let the dust settle before the Russian and Chinese frog is boiled in even hotter water. If Iranian regime change is effectively successful, the United States literally controls the majority of trade. This also has massive implications on the reunification of Taiwan because if the Changs can't fuel their war machine, theyre SOL.

ALTHOUGH what im saying is purely speculative, but if Iran is beaten down enough and the IRGC is out of power, the Ukraine war is also over, due to attrition.

If Russia and China can't get illegal Iranian or Venezuelan oil. It's game over.

62012292
>>62012285
>We already have venezuela's oil under American custodianship that is also severely hindering the world's trade as well
ahahahahahaha. yeah, venezuela will magically fix their non-working oil machinery, re-import their lost expertise, and the shitty venezuelan oil will somehow be refined and distributed in 2 more weeks.

62012296
>>62012292
Thats also a valid assertion given that theyre ran by communists monkeys.

62012444
>>62005061
Trump is the greatest strategist of all time
and by strategist I mean the highest ranked dementia patient of all time

62012471
>>62005061
they're getting desperate

62012484
>>62005061
Wasn't China buying their oil anyway, so this would just mean China could just flip the Iran oil for a profit, what does this do

62012488
>>62012471
>A is the X; B is the Y
Slop detected + opinion discarded

62012490
>>62012471
>gold price will go up because mentally ill, everything-controlling lolbertarian techbros and shit
imagine believing this bullshit lmao

62012498
>>62005206
>what happens
Nukes if they keep going I imagine.

62012504
This reeks of sheer desperation.

62012787
>>62012471
This is some grade-A quality schizo theory and I love it.

62012801
>>62005061
this is meaningless rhetoric and I'm tired of this blatantly bullshit market manipulation every fucking week
the oil was only sanctioned to NATOcucks and Iran got their money in Chinese bank accounts which orange niggers couldn't touch even if he rimmed Netanyahu on an emergency broadcast
no NATOcuck country is going to touch Iranian oil and it will still flow to China so what he's saying is essentially "uhh don't worry guys we won't blow their oil up we know how fucked up things are" which is like NO FUCKING SHIT
I'm so fucking tired of this retarded nigger administration

62013223
>>62005061
Hahahaha these fucking clowns

62013268
I still think nothing ever happens and it'll be okay but Iran and their backers have the once in a generation opportunity to really kick america HARD in the balls

It remains to be seen whether they will want to take it

62013281
>>62005061
it happened yesterday OFAC lifted the restriction through an announcment on their site

62013287
>>62012498
That's not going to bring the oil back

62013304
>>62005061
They are just scared of China involving itself in the war using Iran as a proxy.

62013322
>>62005061
hmmm
maybe they're preparing for taking that island and using that oil

62013373
>>62005061
The goals of the war in iran:
1) Help russia by making oil expensive
2) hurt europe and usa

62013850
>>62012471
This is just fucking dumb and stupid. You'd have to be retarded to believe this is all true.

62013878
>>62012292
what does venezuela need to fix? globocorp can build the infrastructure, and the mcdonalds or amazon private army can be contracted to defend it - or the nypd

62013905
>>62005101
They're not saying they're going to buy it I don't think. They're just not going to put sanctions on other countries for buying it.

62013971
>>62012471
we need to ban aislop posters

62013977
>>62013878
oh yeah, globocorp will rebuild the infra in 2 weeks, and they will do so just because orange man told them "we control their oil!", as if that meant the US will keep control of their oil forever (or for at least 2 more weeks until the next latin american caudillo comes, nationalizes the oil and tells the US to fuck off and die)

62014076
>>62013304
>Its a proxy war between Israel and China, with USA and Iran as the proxies.
I wouldnt have imagined this in 2025

item.title: They flipped the switch — 49 replies — 19.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
They flipped the switch

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-03-21 19:17 UTC

OP

Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point. Well, here it is. The silent flip. Chainlink has permeated the gates of Washington and has considerable influence at this point that was built carefully and mostly out of the spotlight, because regulatory ambiguity around digital assets made overt positioning impossible.

Understand that in groups where powerful men meet and make critical decisions, Chainlink is being positioned as connective infrastructure for the next generation of finance, state-level coordination, and machine-verifiable systems. Monetary policy rails, yes we all know that. But also cyber defense architecture, government identity-adjacent workflows, and potentially even admin systems where the ability to be audited lives in the public forum.

The recent announcement from the SEC/CFTC that deems Chainlink as a commodity is the green light. I can tell you that Economics 2.0 will be announced shortly and the slides are literally being prepared as we speak. The next major milestone after that is staking expansion this summer.

There is still a question about how much friction individual states can still create on the margins (CA, NJ, SC, WI) but the decision makers don't care about that. Hope you don't live in any such places. Most people will not understand what they are looking at until after the repricing. That is how these transitions always work. By the time the headlines catch up, the asymmetry is gone.

Early replies (first 3)

62007047
WW3 is starting and you're still shilling about this gay, irrelevant scamcoin.

62007066
>>62007042
You’re going to have to get a job chief. Typing out your delusions on an indoor kite flying forum isn’t going to pay the bills. Remember, eye contact and firm handshake

62007080
Where's insider anon's larp again?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007938
so when do i sell and at what price

62007973
>>62007905
holy samefag

62008011
>>62007902
ur mom had 3.3 mill inflow today. of semen.
#rekt

62008018
>>62007387
>long-term stakers a bone?
eat shit buddy. Many of us can't even get in the pool. Be happy you've gotten the 4.5% and some random memecoins.

62008356
>>62008018
congratulations on being a latefag. you'll have to make do with sdl

62008363
>>62007042
Finally

My ducks are in a row

62008438
The copium is real in this thread. It's inevitable that we will transition to digital currency and AI oversight. You know it. I know it. It's just a matter of when. And I believe OP's prediction is a rational prediction.

62008468
>$2 above where it was when I first started researching crypto
I figured back then it'd be something I rotated gains into as it seemed to have long-term potential and it seems that's panning out better than I thought.

62008498
>>62007042
>jew id

Holy shit I think this may be a sign

62008529
>>62008498
It's a sign alright

62008569
>>62007042
artemis you suck at larping
you have no panache, no flair for drama
you have no personality

62008595
what the make it by next year stack?

62010029
>>62008569
yeah instead he talks like someone who touches grass (unlike you) and insight (also unlike you), which makes him believable. he's called things right and will be right again dumb fuck

62010118
>>62010029
>omg he's the real insider
i swear atleast one of you fags pop up for every larp. i too knew something was coming, but i kept it to myself because i hate you normal fags and your egos.

62010146
>>62007042
>She still thinks Rome happened on an investment forum
Ya'll deserve to lose money if you dont understand recent history

62010190
>>62010029
he hasn't called shit, shut the fuck up

62012054
>>62010190
>>62010118
check the archive you dummies, oh and touch grass

62012321
>>62012054
It's all fake. Proof is the chart, if any of it were true you would see being priced in.

62012392
>>62012054
>touch grass
you don't even know what your saying newfag

62012758
Price. Chart.
Adem. Won
He won. Linkies lost.

62012774
>>62012321
The longer it moves in lock step with trash like Cardano, the more I doubt.

62012804
>>62007042
> Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point.
thats me. I knew it was march. Its always march. Obvious really.

62012857
>>62012804
Lmaoo it was always X date guys trust le plan

62013253
>>62007042
Right on cue, the next Plato is here to bore us to death on his "theories" and Roman shit

62013315
>>62007042
Artemis, feed us more hopium.

62013349
>>62010118
>>62010029
yeah I've been in every thread telling him he's bad at this.

Cool he gave lukewarm predictions (nothing actionable for the reader) that came true. Now people know that Artemis has some amount of insider information. We don't know his motive, we don't have any reason to believe he wouldn't tell 3 useless truths and then lead everyone off a cliff with an actionable lie.

Artemis actively rejected putting any SOUL into his larp. He wouldn't post a picture of the DC skyline. He didn't post a random trinket like a bar napkin to prove his association with people who make decisions. He is stingy with personal information... cowardly even.

He put nothing on the line. No hints of personality, no connection. Nobody trusts him, nobody cares what he has to say except in hindsight. I think he's either a low level narcissistic staffer who just wants "in on the story of chainlink" for bragging rights. Or he is a sanctioned insider on a mission to achieve some reaction from retail holders. But we don't care what you have to say if you dont play the game.

62013350
>>62013349
Why do you people come here just to LLM post? What's the point nigger?

62013881
>>62013350
nah he's right. the "vibe" is right, but artemis just ain't got no soul.

62014026
>>62007047
It's all pre-planned and pre-organised.
If you can survive and your government doesn't just seize them, you will potentially be very wealthy.
Everything is fake and gay.

item.title: GOLD RUGGED — 68 replies — 17.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
GOLD RUGGED

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-03-21 17:59 UTC

OP

GOLD RUGGED
O
L
D

R
U
G
G
E
D

Early replies (first 3)

61998901
Bitcoin too! Wtf is going on!?

61998912
>>61998888
based quads

61998913
Intense bombings that are destroying crucial infrastructure + FED conference in a few hours. It was obvious

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003858
>>61999058
With the inflation coming, they will have to.

62003865
>>61998901
Look at the volatility on the stock market, with oil moving within a 10% span every day and shit like that.
People are selling their paper gold so they can gamble on the potentially more profitable shit.

62003963
Babbys first red week

62004017
why the fuck is gold dumping? i'm guessing leveraged niggers are deleveraging?
I mean the world is on the brink of collapse why would you dump gold now

62004022
>>62004017
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4hVZOfRW67M

62004042
>>61998888

me finna buy here

62004048
>>62003435
>Texas Tea
Also known as gomutra.

62004272
Just sold everything.

It's over bros. I feels so dumb..

62004291
>>62004017
Dude people are so fucking dumb. "Duhhhhh gold is a safe haven so that means it has to go up when I want it to".

Dipshit - think for a second. Ppl spent years buying gold in case it "all goes to shit" and now it's starting to "all go to shit" and you're shocked they're selling their end of the world hedge? How fucking dumb are you outside of here be fr.

Look at what gold did from 2006-2012. Jesus christ some ppl never fucking even try to learn.

62004296
>>62004017
The end of the modern world was priced in. Thanks for playing. Its just a rock, bro.

62004302
>>62004017
Sry but what really pissed me off was you talking ab deleveraging even though margin requirements have been going down this was really ur fault if u think about it

62004309
>>61999090
I won't have kids but I'll show my niece and nephew my gold purchases from 2021.

62004600
>>62004017
>I mean the world is on the brink of collapse why would you dump gold now
paper gold is worthless for a scenario like that

62005322
Idk how to dollar cost average so whatever I put in I just buy by the percentage it went down

62005367
>>62004600
>paper

62008264
>>61998888
gold is unruggable, small fluctuations like this dont matter

62009069
>>61998888
nice quads retard. also this points to the big players trying to close the exits by putting up a smoke screen. the system is imploding and they don't want anyone running for actual hard assets or assets outside of the system. this is a warning from them showing their hand. they are scared as fuck.

the rips we saw in January were breaches in a the dam. they are trying to shore that up with tape. it's still leaking.

62009076
>>62008264
>gold is unruggable
this in absolute terms. but in the short terms it easily shakes off paper handed retards who babysit the ticker and shit themselves when the price drops 3% and immediately sell like the losers they are.

62009209
just buy the dips

62009994
I literally put everything outside of my 401k to this a month ago. Keep with it or move to SGOV? Holy fuck, thought I'd at least make a bit more playing it safe with it

62010048
>>62008264
>linear chart meant to fuel FOMO

62010070
>>61998888
checked

62010074
>>61998888
okbye

62010178
>>62000000

62010196
>>61999999

62011319
>>62010048
>posts the silver chart

Fuck off heeb

62011372
>>62004017
>I mean the world is on the brink of collapse
People hoard gold for the end of the world, anon. They think the world is ending, so now they are selling their gold. This is the end result of buying gold.

62011388
>>62010178

62013826
>>62010048
>>62011319
Here's the gold one, adjusted for inflation.

62013889
>>61998888
Gold is meant for you to go in-and-out of. It's money. That's why the bid-ask spread is so tiny compared to other assets. Compare that to when real estate rugs and the bid-ask gets blown the fuck out.

item.title: BATURDAY: SUICIDE HOTLINE EDITION — 4 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BATURDAY: SUICIDE HOTLINE EDITION

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-21 21:32 UTC

OP

FULL SPEED AHEAD TO ZERO
POST THOTS TO HELP COPE

Early replies (first 3)

62014156
You know what's funny is back in december 2020 I was awake at 3:59 AM when Musk tweeted Doge and it began to take off. Just 10k in that would have made me millions. Instead I set up my laptop to passively 'mine' BAT by automatically getting ads every day like a jeet. Kek. Lol.

My problem is that I was in crypto in 2013, decided it was useless for anything besides Drugs, and fucked off for 5 years until 2018 when I saw it being ponzi'd by USDT, then fucked off again for 3 years until it was almost 2021. The concept of 'crypto for the tech' appeals to me, but the markets now aren't and haven't been about the tech for years, so it was a bad match. If I was just a dipshit buying Hype Thing tweeted by McAfee or Musk I would have been rich. Maybe I would have roundtripped it, but that would be better than trying to mine fucking BAT lmao.

62014174
>>62014156
bat cucked us SO HARD

62014349
>>62014156
I escaped after apple banned it, no, i am not an iphone nigger, but after years of waiting for a fucking decent self seve ad dashboard, i got bored and I knew this tumor weeks tactic is just smoke and mirrors plus they are selling the from the user growth pool(or wtf) on huobi

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62014358
>>62014349
I switch all my stack into eth, eth was 500$ for the last time at that day

item.title: Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore. — 14 replies — 10.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-21 20:34 UTC

OP

Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.
>at the $15 million and up price point the preference is doorman style apartment buildings, especially if this is a residence and not an investment
Why?

Early replies (first 3)

62013834
Tartaria..

62013851
>>62013830
All the ultra luxury housing in NY is speculative investment. No one lives in those fancy new high rises much less in these "mansions".

62013855
why don't the sellers lower the price

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62013874
>nobody wants to buy
Plenty of people want to buy, they just don't want to lower the price
>I know what I have

62014099
>>62013830
>live in NYC mansion
>ride subway to get anywhere
>get stabbed by monkeys
If you have money there are 15 million better places to live.

62014132
why are people not lining up for the nyc mansions? do they truly prefer 300sqft studios?
it's hard to understand people. a mystery really

62014160
>>62013830
Panic room sure was a movie.

62014163
Security.

62014169
>>62013830
It surprises me that anyone with enough money to see a world outside of New York City would still willingly live there, much less spend millions on these mansions... Does a 1,000 acre ranch in the midwest not meet their homeless nigger per square foot quota? Maybe not enough literal garbage and piss smells to feel like home? I really don't get these "people."

62014184
For people with that much money, jurisdiction matters more than location.

62014197
>>62013830
Give me $50M and I'll buy one

62014199
>>62014169
rich liberals love the hustle and the bustle
doubt theres any bums loitering around there

62014207
>>62014197
If someone gives you $50M now, you will try to gamble it on shitcoins, you know, to double it. And lose it all to a DEX scam.

62014208
>>62014169
>>62014099
Trying to decide if I want to explain or if I just trust my instincts that these are Israeli glowies.

item.title: WHAT THE FUCK HAPPENED TO OIL? — 11 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT THE FUCK HAPPENED TO OIL?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-21 20:47 UTC

OP

I WAS CAMPING IN A FOREST WITH NO INTERNET FOR 1 MONTH

Early replies (first 3)

62011185
>>62011182
WW3 anon

62011213
straits closed due to aids

62011237
>>62011182
Trump (Obviously Scott Bessent) is wanting to choke the rest of the world's oil supply and by extension—industrial and manufacturing capacity. All while we remain relatively unscathed and eventually come up on top because of our utilization of newly acquired Venezuelan oil, hence the invasion weeks before.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62011278
>>62011182
imagine if this were real and you just go dark for a month cause shits gay and nothing ever happens and come back to a geopolitical crisis

62011395
king migger stroked out and shit himself

62011668
>>62011278
>imagine if this were real and you just go dark for a month cause shits gay and nothing ever happens and come back to a geopolitical crisis
I knew a guy that went on a digital detox with only a house phone and didn't hear about Charlie Kirk getting shot until two weeks after the fact when one of my socially retarded friends showed him some AI slop meme of Charlie Kirk and George Kirk robocop'd

62011681
>>62011182
>camping in a forest
>using some sort of oil product as fuel/heat
>how could this happen to me1!!!!1!1!

62013823
Iran is bombing everyone's refineries after it got its own refineries bombed. America ran out of missiles to shoot down the drones and missiles so if Iran chooses to, they can just delete an irreplaceable refinery.

62013961
>>62013823
It's hard for me to believe something built in the 1970s or 1980s is irreplaceable.

62014201
>>62013961
That's what every late stage civilization says before death. Even boomers.
>my hip bone was built in the womb in 1946, how can it be irreplacab-ACK

62014234
>>62011681
Maybe he had a magnifying glass

item.title: Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake — 54 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:06 UTC

OP

I’ve been telling folks here not to sell BTC and now they’re buying at the top.

>When are you gonna learn your lesson, folks? HODL

Early replies (first 3)

61996853
>>61996755
rich kid but black

61996883
>>61996853
What's the problem?

61998635
>>61996755
Sell and wait for 10k per btc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003136
>>62003088
It's 0.07% APR. And you will be sending your BTC to an anonymous system whose owner pinky promises to send you your money back if you ask nicely.

62003422
>>62003088
It’s possible, just look into it yourself. you’ll get it. Self custody is the advantage.

62003430
>>61996755
Let the goyim deal in the crypto world.

62003444
>>62003136
“Anonymous system” lol. It’s literally built around cryptographic guarantees, not trust. APR is low, sure, but better than nothing. This isn’t sending BTC to some random yield farm.

62003448
>>61998652
He's a great kiddo

62003660
>>62003430
Bitcoin keeps improving. Lightning, Taproot, and other upgrades make BTC faster, cheaper, and more usable, confidence grows, price follows.

62003668
Market sentiment drives crypto hard. A little media buzz, some whales buying, and suddenly BTC is pumping. FOMO is real.

62003679
>>62003444
The only defi right now is ethereum

62003695
>>62003117
Whats your price target?

62003910
>>62003679
“Only Ethereum” is outdated. Solana and Sui already have real DeFi, and BTC is just getting started. Most of it is still sleeping, not because it can’t be used.

62003914
>>62003111
Smart guy for buying the dip

62004044
>>61996755
I am blaming Trump for cryoto dumps

62004059
your president is greedy ass

62004175
>>61996755
>Buy XRP and LINK instead

62004400
>>61996755
I need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. your exit will come at some point in the future, trust the plan.

62005462
>>62003660

62006331
>>62003668

62006772

62007988

62008412

62008424

62008434

62010261

62010288

62011895

62011911
That's not link staking, that's to link your staking account to get the trading discounts.

In anycase, it's sad to see the state of biz. The most used blockchain, the main RWA chain, literally partnering to get S&P on chain (instantly deployed after the announcement), built in the USA btw, and the dinocoin holders still call you jeet.

Let's go, polkadot! Let's go, cardano! Jesuschrist

62011920
>>62011911

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62011944
>>62011920
Why do you lie? It's literally the most used chain right now.

It's becoming what all the other chains wanted to be. But it's up to you, keep waiting for the cardano dapps, or the next Eth update in 2028. I don't care, but it's sad for the new people that come to biz and swallow up your lies.

62013336
>>62011944

what can you even do on starknet?

62013346
>>62011944
Link literally runs on Ethereum. The slave-suckers hate to hear it, but Link is just an Ethereum token.

62013361
>>62013346
no no it's chain agnostic!
it can move at anytime
I remember asking what would happen to chainlink if ethereum stopped working many times and all anyone ever told me was "that won't happen"

62014236

>Centralized """validators"""
>Shilled by literally every grifter (Arthur/KOLs) and VC scums

item.title: Kaspa Schizo Thread — 3 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kaspa Schizo Thread

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-03-21 21:13 UTC

OP

Kaspa just passed 1B total transactions.

Daily transactions average 5M now.

Some entity is still actively stress testing the network.

Kaspa is the ONLY crypto that merges PoS Tx capability with PoW security.

Use this information as you will.

Early replies (first 3)

62014187
>>62014155

62014211
>>62014155
>Some entity is still actively stress testing the network.
Reality: There is an Israeli bot network spamming millions of useless transactions per day. Thats what you get for having 0 transaction cost.

62014319
>>62014155
looks organic, i aped livesaving saar!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Bitcoin is being replaced — 28 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin is being replaced

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-21 15:49 UTC

OP

This is, with my whole hearted belief, the next Bitcoin.

Last year there were murmurs here of Bitcoin being set to be artificially under attack in 2026 in order to make way for a biometric UBI blockchain.

All wars are bankers wars.

Anon posted a website that since went down (kingtree.net) and had the archives scrubbed. On it was a forum from 2006 to 2009 that laid out the original code and implementation of Bitcoin. The code was, per the post and forum, stolen and commodified. Satoshi was there.

At the same time, the url ankh.cash was registered and dormant. Same name of the kingtree protocol (ANKH). The blockchain running since 2006 went offline, but is now live and open source on the ankh.cash url. It's been made public on February 28 2026 at 3:33am ET (numerology relevance) on Github.

I've monitored it since, and over the past two weeks, at the start of the Iran and Israel war, it began verifying biometrics and users onto the chain and distributing.

I downloaded a node and went through the biometric data. From my analysis and attempts at deciphering - it is being implemented in early stages in South America, South East Asia, and East Africa - the global south. Based on phenotypes of facial ratios.

The financial system is set to be imploded on purpose to make way for Ankh and its derivatives. Biometric blockchain based UBI for the masses - one code, one hash and tokenization of people's entire livelihood.

Early replies (first 3)

62008048
>>62008039
big if true

62008050
>>62008039
Ahhh man
*checks pants*
Yeah I've got some nasty poopie in there.
*Takes pants off and tips them upside down*
Ahhh man now it's all in your thread.

Financially speaking I this will decimate me and that's why this is on topic.

62008079
keep me posted

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62008083
>>62008039
>>62008048
>>62008050
>>62008079

samefag

62008088
>>62008083
fagsame

62008092
>>62008088
famesag

62008099
>>62008083
all me

62008108
>>62008092
sagfame

62008109
>>62008083
You're pointing and screaming into the void

62008323
>>62008039
When bitcoin came out, it wasn't the next anything, it was itself.

62008333
>>62008039
>capped at 9 billion
I thought it was 500m

62008341
>>62008039
Yeah say hello to those who understand face training, bone health, hormones and the light angles...woman can even play with makep and face fillers too...this cannot be just that simple

62008358
Ok so Whats the ticker ANKH is like $200 mcap cant buy it on SOL

62008512
>>62008358
>buy it
buy it? as a goy? ahahaha
you vill have to work for zem

62008699
theres literally no point to buying this because it literally says its to allocate 2.7 million ie 1 ankh per person via biometric identification.

Sorry but you have a critical flaw in your shillcoin, theres no reason to buy

62009087
>>62008333
0.0
Can't tell if blessed or cursed digits

62012161
I spent an entire day's worth of compute power decoding, deciphering and reconstructing the biometric data of the first person verified on this blockchain.

Node details:

"index": 159,
"timestamp": 1772259107285.0525,
"hash": "0xc697fe256c7ec05764ad5822dd845e41d0c91b7848c01d680f1327c98ecb88a1",
"previousHash": "0x17d347a607e5c6561601a8e122f2b507cf3972ef0ee0868f2c494e9c533e34d8",
"validator": "system",
"stateRoot": "0x098d578a30f40e43f9348c5d63aef8cce289c28faabd7ca98f84c49c71167b6a",
"transactionsRoot": "0xe182ae9f084ec96a4a32f5693c97cc0643748cc4d33cd30ecd9bf3976c975c26",
"receiptsRoot": null,
"consensusType": "SYSTEM",
"sidechainId": null,
"transactions": [
{
"id": "2646f826-a0e5-4208-96e5-aa9540cbcd9a",
"type": "BIOMETRIC_REGISTRATION",
"from": "ankh_a5e4c5a16e2355556116252dcfa0a63466f6e0a3",
"to": "system",
"value": "0",
"fee": "0",
"nonce": 0,
"data": {
"biometricHash": "932a461e62200978f15a8643ba0e4b06258864ca5d8e5558d1db03cdce2367dc",
"biometricTemplateHash": "932a461e62200978f15a8643ba0e4b06258864ca5d8e5558d1db03cdce2367dc"

62012192
Just tell me where to buy it fag

62012289
Another one. These reconstructions are approximations based on the biometric data. These few are likely individuals in the shadows. Looks a bit like Bill Ackman. Would like to see if anyone else can reconstruct this from the data and get the same image.

{
"id": "f8de47b6-d2f1-4021-a222-f46e0fd48a5f",
"type": "BIOMETRIC_REGISTRATION",
"from": "ankh_f585c14699e6a5aaac7ff3a0fdf8427dc88c6fab",
"to": "system",
"value": "0",
"fee": "0",
"nonce": 0,
"data": {
"biometricHash": "762aa1edc2221af8ec73731e40e25747571acdc5e8f94e2f7bbc37b36dc68d8c",
"biometricTemplateHash": "762aa1edc2221af8ec73731e40e25747571acdc5e8f94e2f7bbc37b36dc68d8c"

62012688
>>62008323
And then came XMR.

62013151
MASS VERIFICATION EVENT DETECTED

Uptick in verifications. Investigating and reporting back with updates.

62013172
>>62008039
>This is, with my whole hearted belief, the next Bitcoin.
-t every shitcoin baggot ever

62013176
>>62012688
based

62013395
Confirmed implementation in the Horn of Africa - Bab Al-Mandab Strait. The mass verification event came from there.

What this means, and my analysis:

A sidechain built on the Ankh blockchain is being implemented in the north coast of Somalia with a separatist or sovereign movement verifying biometrics for the population in the region and issuing currency to them.

With the conflict in Iran and the Strait of Hormuz disruptions, somebody is playing their cards and placing their bets on gaining control over another strait in the region, potentially controlling the currency supply and asserting control over the trade routes.

I'm still digging, but this is very interesting and gives credence to my hypothesis. Much of this may have been planned in the last months of 2025. Biometric data elsewhere in the chain also points to Argentina being verified and active on the mainnet.

62013469
The .cash domain extension wasn't added until 2014

You are a faggot

62013472
>>62008039
>This new cryptocurrency will replace Bitcoin and will be equally distributed across every person in the world
>Anyways, here's where you can go buy as much as you want - before it moons!

62013631
>>62008039
>look guys i repackaged worldcoin. Give me your eyeball scans!

62013636
>>62008109
Leave the void-screamers alone

item.title: >LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU this generation will be the most broke in history. — 25 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU this generation will be the most broke in history.

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-21 16:27 UTC

OP

>LOAN FORGIVENESS FOR ME BUT NOT FOR YOU
this generation will be the most broke in history.
who will buy your stocks and crypto now?

Early replies (first 3)

62013196
loans wouldn't be a problem if interest was paid after the principle

literally all loan-related problems are from people upset they've payed $40k and only $4k of that went towards the principle

62013204
>>62013196
How about instead we tell the dumb niggers that get mad at this that the interest is paying astronauts to build a wall in space to protect them from aliens?

62013206
>>62013196
How about we just ban usury (again?)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62013208
>>62013206
if you can convince the libertarians that a 40% interest rate isn't doing anyone any favors then you have my support

62013229
>>62013196
Would that look so much better?

>I paid 200k of my 250k loan but still have to pay 200k in interest after the principal

62013237
>>62012736
the high IQ move is to get on an income driven repayment scheme (RAP or IBR) and choose to manually re-certify your income (i.e., don't use your most recent tax forms) with minimum wage photoshopped pay stubs.

>t-that's illegal!!! you'll get caught!!!!

62013328
>>62012736
>Nooo I took out a loan and signed my name on the contract and everything why am I being expected to honor the contract that's not faaaaaiiiiir
You did it to yourselves. Now grow the fuck up and pay it back.

62013334
>>62013237
The high IQ move would have been to convince the university to give you enough scholarships and fellowships that there was nothing left for you pay.

62013341
>>62013334
it's state dependent. i graduated with a state university (in pennsylvania) with a 3.8 GPA in applied math. never once received a dime of scholarship aid.

62013359
>>62013196
Huh? Extra payments each month should be applied to your principal amount. Mathematically, paying an extra couple hundred here and there actually make a big difference over the life of a loan. As far as total interest goes, anyway.
If your bank is not applying extra money towards principal, you're getting super-jewed. Like, that's a whole other level of usury lol.

62013368
>>62013341
>it's state dependent.
Nope. It's very IQ dependent though. You get offered those scholarships and fellowships in high school. By the time you actually show up to university it's too late, you're already enrolled and paying your own way and they have nothing to gain by throwing money at you. They're an enticement for high IQ prospects, not a reward for studying hard.

62013372
>>62012736
Car salesman here

It won’t matter. Retards will always find a way.
You can try to talk them down to more reasonable purchase (doesn’t matter to me, in fact the cheaper the vehicle usually the better the profit excepting the absolute max platinum super high rollers package).

They won’t hear of it.
They want their AC seats and they will go 20% interest rolling 15k to do it, and they will do it with you or find someone else.

62013377
>>62013359
He's saying he wants all of each payment to go towards the principle. Which he can totally do, but that has to be written into the loan agreement. It's his failure, but that won't stop him from whining about how "unfair" it is that the contract he read and signed is now suddenly not to his particular preferences, rather than simply paying down the principle at an accelerated rate by paying more than the absolute minimum each month Because whining is free and easy and doesn't involve difficult things like adulting.

62013510
>>62013377
Yeah let me just call up the Department of Education and negotiate a unique loan agreement. Some people don't have any good options. Doesn't mean we should let international bankers prey on them.

62013544
>>62013368
no, it's literally dependent on how your state funds public schools. florida, for example, has a very generous and easy to get "bright stars" scholarship. pennsylvania has no such program and comparatively expensive state schools, because they chose to underfund colleges.

what's the deal with kinky haired muttalo zoomers running off their big ugly gorilla lips when they haven't the slightest idea?

62013583
>>62013196
This. Usury has killed mankind. We live in a parasite state and world

62013593
>>62013196
Loans wouldn't be a problem if people didn't take out loans with larger monthly payments than they could afford to pay.
The problem is not the system, the problem is people's financial literacy.
Or lack thereof.

62013624
>>62013593
>Loans wouldn't be a problem if people didn't take out loans with larger monthly payments than they could afford to pay.

this argument is meaningless when you look at how quickly certain fields of study can be "disrupted." software development was almost a sure bet if you weren't retarded. within 5 years it was destroyed by a combination of outsourced indians, AI, corporations funding bootcamps, and the media attracting students. similar things have happened in law, accounting, and pharmacy. similar things are in the works for certain areas of medicine and dentistry.

62013630
>>62013206
Economy slows down without usury.
>heh GOOD
Yeah except all other countries won't be slowed down and your retarded ass country will be lapped and left behind in no time. And then probably raped and conquered and erased from history. It's a doggy dog world

62013635
>>62012736
i don't understand the op pic. is there a point in this screenshot? explain please

62013677
>>62013328
The f35 Iran is blowing up costs 200 billion dollars

62013683
>>62013196
That’s the risk premium of borrowing because too many retards default on their loans.

You’re more than welcome to lend out your own money at a lower interest rate. See how long it takes before you go bankrupt

62013690
>>62013683
They can't default on student loans

62013712
>>62013196
>>62012736
Fafsa loans shouldn'thave interest....
Why do people not understand that ALL FEDERLA FUNDING COMES FROM TAXPAYERS

If you are paying a loan and paying taxes you are being taxed twice. All loans should be either tax write offs or credits lmao. The audacity of a country to create such a fucking up education system, then outsource/insource H1bs to jobs, then say "sorry goy, you gotta be taxed twice".

No one would care about repayment if the system simply acknowledged double taxation is illegal and I will go tea party on your asses if it isn't fixed.

62013713
>>62013208
exterminate lolbergs and trannies, they're 1%~ of the population, then fix the system without freaks and pedos.

>>62013368
t. has a low iq

item.title: Power of the ICP — 13 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Power of the ICP

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-21 18:41 UTC

OP

Made a rouge-lite space shooter arcade game on the ICP with caffeine AI. ICP to $1,000!!!

-3 ships to choose from with unique abilities and play style
-Dozens of upgrades
-cool power ups
-bosses
-leader boards
-enemy variety
-25 waves to beat the game
-prestige levels for harder and harder challenges.

This is fleshed out and polished game you can have fun with for 2-3 hrs. Works really good on tablet and smart phone. Desktop not fully tested.

https://pixel-blaster-vew.caffeine.xyz/#caffeineAdminToken=868f11e8852ffbb163253ee5f585647f53b0ebc21fdd8a314cd9c94210b2acfa

Early replies (first 3)

62007884
Nice

62007899
>>62007867
cool game, icp isnt going to pump tho. glad youre having fun making stuff, thanks for sharing.

62010116
>>62007867
I beat wave 7 on a trackpad
make it harder
remove ablity to move up and down make it like space invaders
it's pretty imbalanced as an arcade game but better than trash on icp. so keep up the good work. play some early 80s games to develop your design sensibilities.
how do you make money?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62012224
>>62010116
Waves get much harder later on, play on hard mode if too easy and unlock prestige levels by beating wave 25.

Game turns into a bullet hell and yo need to be able to move anywhere

62012231
>>62012224
Later prestige levels the early waves get difficult to kill all the enemies and you lose your “enemy clear” bonus and bonus 3rd upgrade pick. Early waves are about eliminating all enemies and getting the bonuses

62012426
>>62007867
Tech two decades ahead of time.
Yet nobody uses it, and it will fail.
It's the General Motors EV1 of blockchain.
Too much ahead of it's time.

62012450
lmfao

62012466
>>62007867
people laugh, but i'm start to believe that as internet and OS become more and more kiked with ID laws, Internet computer will become the last bastion of freedom.

62012543
Price chart cope sneethe dial 8

62012575
>>62007867
he bought something called
>internet computer
>i see pee

62012706
>>62012466
Perhaps. But it will not pump the price.
Okay let's give some advice.
Nobody gives a shit about the tech. Any tech. If it's ahead of it's time. Whatever. What count is the market. What does the market want.
Currently, it wants two things. One, it's starting to realize the importance of privacy (anonymous transactions). Secondly, it wants a very fast, and cheap (transaction fees) blockchain, to do mostly stable coin (which is a gateway to DOLLARS, not their local hyper inflated currency, in the third world).

62013913
>>62012231
make it harder faster
play level 1 of phoneix on mame you'll see what I mean

62013955
Whoop

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 87 replies — 3.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-03-20 22:50 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446

That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

61998660
>>61932834

This shit is going to 100 dollars isnt it

61998672
>>61997626
Oh shit anon. We should've told you that if YOU buy then it's going to 0$. You should sell and then after you sell we'll GIGA ULTRA MEGA PUMP to 100$+

61998866
>>61998660
Yes. Yes it is

61999089
>>61998866
Did you buy?

62000247
>>61999089
Of course. 100 DOT holder reporting in

62000433
>>62000247
It's over guys. This guy doomed us. Because he bought it's going to 0$. Please sell before I put my DOT into my cars brakes.

62004150
>>62000247
The moment you bough the whole market started dumping.

62007000
100$ end of bull run. Is it possible to keep the thread alive for 1 full month? 15 days to go!

62007009
How much are they paying you jeets to post here?

62007046
>>62007009
Well... The question is... How much do you want to get paid to jeet like we do? We can easily mog your jobs salary. JEETMAXXING

62007254
>>62007009
Zero. Plus im white

62008295
>>62007254
Good one dear fren Rajakesh Pujakistan. That'll convince him. Now post your white hand with a timestamp. Picrel is me and my white hand sar

62010139
So... When's JAM coming out guys? Those fuckers stopped doing the monthly development calls

62010191
>>62010139
If you're not a bot, here's a question: why constantly humiliate yourself in public like this? It's embarrassing. Nobody cares about the pedochain and Gayvin.

62010262
>>62010191
I'm really glad you brought that up anon. Great question! And if I want to answer it properly, like you deserve, my good sar, then I'll need a bit more information
>why constantly humiliate yourself in public like this? It's embarrassing. Nobody cares about the pedochain and Gayvin.
What do you mean by constantly humiliate myself? Can you help me understand that

62011878
